name: Z-Image-Turbo Local Image Generation
description: Local AI image generation working on AMD ROCm GPU, integrated into blog + content pipeline
gate: Generate 3 test images locally, blog pipeline uses local gen for hero images

userStories.title: Verify Z-Image-Turbo workflow runs on ROCm AMD GPU
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need local image generation working on my AMD GPU so I can generate images at $0 marginal cost.
acceptanceCriteria: ComfyUI starts successfully with --lowvram flag on AMD GPU
Z-Image-Turbo workflow JSON loads without errors in ComfyUI
Generate one test image (512x512) successfully
GPU utilization shows activity during generation (rocm-smi)
VRAM usage stays under 14GB during generation
Output image saved to disk and viewable
userStories.notes: ComfyUI already installed at ~/ComfyUI with comfy-venv. Z-Image-Turbo models already downloaded (20GB). May need PyTorch ROCm build check.

userStories.title: Download and integrate ControlNet model for Z-Image-Turbo
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need ControlNet support for guided image generation (pose, depth, edges) so I can create consistent brand imagery.
acceptanceCriteria: ControlNet model Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union.safetensors downloaded to ComfyUI models dir
ControlNet workflow JSON loads in ComfyUI
Generate one test image with ControlNet guidance
ControlNet improves image composition vs unguided generation

userStories.title: Smoke test: generate 3 sample images (photoreal, illustration, text-render)
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need to verify Z-Image-Turbo handles multiple styles so I can use it for blog hero images, social media, and marketing.
acceptanceCriteria: Photorealistic image: generate a professional tech workspace photo
Illustration: generate a clean infographic-style illustration
Text rendering: generate an image with English text overlay that is readable
All 3 images generated in under 60 seconds each
Images saved to ~/test-output/ with naming convention: style_timestamp.png
userStories.notes: Z-Image-Turbo supports bilingual text rendering (EN/CN). Test specifically for text quality.

userStories.title: Create Hermes skill for Z-Image-Turbo image generation
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a reusable Hermes skill for local image generation so that any agent/cron can generate images without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Skill file created at skills/creative/comfyui/ with Z-Image-Turbo workflow instructions
Skill includes prompt templates for blog hero, social media, thumbnail, illustration
Skill documents the API call pattern (POST to ComfyUI /prompt endpoint)
Skill includes troubleshooting for common ROCm/AMD issues
Skill tested by generating an image via Hermes agent call
userStories.notes: Existing ComfyUI skill at skills/creative/comfyui/SKILL.md — extend or create sub-skill.

userStories.title: Integrate local image gen into blog pipeline (replace cloud fallback)
userStories.description: As the blog automation operator, I need the blog pipeline to generate hero images locally instead of using cloud ZAI glm-image so I can reduce costs to $0/image.
acceptanceCriteria: Blog pipeline cron job uses local ComfyUI for hero image generation
Cloud ZAI glm-image remains as fallback if local gen fails
Generated hero images match blog post topic (relevant visual)
Images saved to public/images/blog/ in WebP format
Pipeline still completes end-to-end (blog post + image + build + deploy)
Cost per blog post drops from $0.015 to $0 for image generation
userStories.notes: Current blog pipeline uses ZAI glm-image cloud API. Replace with local gen, keep cloud as fallback.

userStories.title: Benchmark: local vs cloud image generation
userStories.description: As a decision-maker, I need performance and quality benchmarks comparing local vs cloud image generation so I can make informed decisions about which to use.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate 10 images locally and 10 via cloud (same prompts)
Measure: generation time, image quality (subjective 1-10), VRAM usage, cost
Write benchmark report to ralph/phase1-benchmark.md
Recommendation: when to use local vs cloud based on results
userStories.notes: This data informs future infrastructure decisions and skill defaults.

userStories.title: Create image style presets for Pepewebtech brand consistency
userStories.description: As a brand operator, I need consistent visual style across all generated images so that Pepewebtech has a cohesive brand look.
acceptanceCriteria: Define 5 style presets: blog-hero, social-card, thumbnail, infographic, testimonial-bg
Each preset has: prompt template, negative prompt, seed range, dimensions, style keywords
Presets saved in skill references/ directory
Test each preset: generate one image per preset, verify brand consistency
Document presets in skill file
userStories.notes: Pepewebtech brand: modern, clean, tech-forward, dark/light theme, bilingual-friendly.
